topic: Distance-redshift Cepheid/SBF — Self-Contradicted under MOND-grouping reclassification (c3 verdict-flip verification + carry-over check)
change_summary: verdict changed from 'Misleading' (c3 fingerprint) to 'Self-Contradicted' (current) per EXP-295 integration commit 5603fce1 on 2026-05-10. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-16T01:10:33Z (45h old, within freshness window). priority_bucket=verdict_changed. obe_skipped=2 (WIN-052/054 both already re-reviewed since the audit was generated). No drifted_fields beyond verdict — text content is nearly identical to c3 fingerprint. Cycle 4 verifies (a) the verdict-flip integration is clean, (b) c3 carry-over holes (Part 6.13 non-integration, sub_rationale non-rendering, EXP-295 8,619 km wording) are tracked, and (c) the SC verdict still holds under fresh adversarial attack.
our_argument_summary: WIN-055 now sits at verdict 'Self-Contradicted' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:verdict). The substantive argument: the dome cites Cepheid and SBF standard-candle measurements that place galaxies at 10–100 Mpc, while the dome's own published geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km; firmament_height=9,086 km) provides no spatial framework where such distances can exist. Three independent stellar-physics distance rungs (Cepheids, TRGB, SBF — each tied to a different physical process) converge on these distance scales, so the SC argument is not dependent on accepting any one calibration. The sub_rationale 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' frames this as the fifth and final WIN in a coordinated EXP-295 reclassification covering WIN-047/048/052/054/055, all reframed from Misleading/Not-Demonstrated to Self-Contradicted on the same closed-world / bounded-firmament logic.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automation. WIN-055 is a narrative claim citing standard-candle distance scales; no monitor.py domain processes any Cepheid/SBF observation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — no monitor.py entry. The dome's claim is purely citational (it points at the literature distance ladder), not algorithmic.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None. The dome's claim is a static citation of the cosmic distance ladder, not a live-monitored prediction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our detail_evidence does not make any code-execution claims about this WIN.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2/c3 and re-verified: monitor.py contains no domain handling Cepheid period-luminosity, SBF magnitudes, or TRGB tip-of-branch luminosities. The dome's automation does not fetch or compare any standard-candle data; the WIN is a pure narrative citation of the established distance ladder.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward and re-verified. The Cepheid PL relation (Leavitt 1912) and the SBF magnitude calibration (Tonry & Schneider 1988; Blakeslee et al. 2021) are standard stellar physics. The dome adds a renaming layer (z=D/λ_A in place of v=H0·D) but no new mechanism for either pulsation periods or surface-brightness magnitude–distance relations.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward and re-verified. Cepheid distance-ladder values predate the dome model by ~100 years (Hubble 1929 distance to M31; Sandage 1958 mature ladder; Freedman et al. 2001 HST Key Project; Riess et al. 2022 SH0ES). The dome adopted the published distances and labeled them confirmation. No prospective prediction or pre-publication timestamp exists.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward and re-verified. No derivation chain from disc_radius=20,015 km / firmament_height=9,086 km / sun_altitude=5,733 km / moon_altitude=2,534 km arrives at any Cepheid or SBF distance. The dome's claim is value-adoption, not value-derivation.

holes_found.description: 'Magellanic parallax' wording is technically inaccurate. The detail_evidence contains two phrases — 'Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax' and 'anchored to Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence) — that conflate two distinct anchor mechanisms. The LMC's distance is NOT measured via direct trigonometric parallax (the LMC is at ~50 kpc; parallax of LMC stars from Gaia DR3 is at the noise floor and contributes only weakly to the LMC distance scale). The LMC distance is primarily established via detached eclipsing binaries (Pietrzynski et al. 2013 Nature 495, 76; Pietrzynski et al. 2019 Nature 567, 200), achieving 1% precision. Cepheid PL calibration uses two independent anchors: (a) Milky Way Cepheids with Gaia DR3 parallaxes (~1% — this is the parallax the detail_evidence correctly mentions one sentence earlier) and (b) LMC Cepheids tied to the DEB distance. So 'Magellanic parallax' is a real geometric measurement only in the very weak Gaia-DR3-LMC sense; the load-bearing LMC anchor is eclipsing binaries. A knowledgeable defender will spot this and use it to claim our review garbles the distance-ladder fundamentals.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'anchored to Magellanic Cloud geometric parallax' with 'anchored to Milky Way Cepheid Gaia parallaxes and the LMC distance from detached eclipsing binaries (Pietrzynski et al. 2019)'. Replace 'via Magellanic parallax' in the trailing sentence with 'via the LMC detached-eclipsing-binary anchor (Pietrzynski et al. 2019) and Milky Way Cepheid Gaia parallaxes'. Adds one citation (Pietrzynski 2019, DOI:10.1038/s41586-019-0999-4) but eliminates a nitpick a defender could otherwise use to discredit the paragraph.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c3 hole #1, still unresolved: the WIN-055.sub_rationale field ('category-error import (MOND grouping): dome z=D/λ_A formula is Hubble Law rewritten with renamed constant; no aetheric pulsation or SBF mechanism' — data/wins.json:WIN-055:sub_rationale) is NOT rendered on the published site. Verified by grep of build.js and build-scripts/generate-html.js — neither file references sub_rationale as a rendered field, and docs/index.html does not contain the sub_rationale text. A reader hitting WIN-055 on the live site sees the 'Self-Contradicted' verdict label with NO surfaced rationale explaining why a Cepheid/SBF distance WIN got that verdict. The c3 review pointed at ISS-1922 as tracking this rendering question, but ISS-1922 (closed 2026-05-09) is actually about a WIN-033 post-hoc paragraph wording — it does not track sub_rationale rendering at all. The 'See Part 6.13' cross-reference inside sub_rationale also fails because Part 6.13 has not been integrated (see hole #3). This is the same defect on WIN-047/048/052/054, but those have separate review cycles; flagging on WIN-055 because c3 raised it and decider should now resolve it on all five at once.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should EITHER (i) create a new open-issue to either wire sub_rationale rendering into the WIN panel (preferred — the field exists in data and a reader of a Self-Contradicted verdict has a legitimate need to understand the structural argument) OR (ii) document the audit-trail-only convention in CLAUDE.md and add an in-prose explanatory sentence to detail_verdict_text on all five reclassified WINs pointing readers to Part 6.13 (which then also needs to actually exist — see hole #3). The current state — verdict label without surfaced rationale, with a cross-reference to a section that doesn't exist — is the worst of all worlds.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c3 hole #0, still unresolved with new wrinkle: Part 6.13 (the EXP-295 cross-cutting MOND-grouping subsection) is NOT integrated into sections.json. Verified by grep of all part1..part10 html blobs — no '6.13', no 'category-error import', no 'MOND grouping' prose, no 'imported in late March 2026' sentinel from the EXP-295 proposed_subsection_html body. The EXP-295 expansion file has status=null and integrated_at=null (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json). The five verdict-flip patches inside EXP-295 WERE applied (commits 1cfdafef + 5603fce1, the latter explicitly noting 'Part 6.13 not yet integrated'). The 8,619 km wording-fix HNOTE (HNOTE-OPERATOR-EXP-295-MOND-8619-WORDING-FIX-001) was applied to the EXP file on 2026-05-10 (visible in wording_fix_2026_05_10 field), so the c3 hole #0 numerical error is FIXED in the source expansion. But the section itself has never been integrated, so neither the wording fix nor the cross-cutting argument has reached the live site. This is the audit-trail home that WIN-055.sub_rationale ('See Part 6.13') points to — and it doesn't exist on the rendered site.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should either (i) push EXP-295 to the priority queue with target_type='section-new' so the cross-cutting subsection actually gets integrated, OR (ii) remove the 'See Part 6.13' reference from all five sub_rationales and pick a different surfaced-rationale strategy (e.g., a paragraph in Part 5 Kill Shots, or an inline detail_verdict_text addendum). Two months of partial integration with a sub_rationale pointer to a non-existent section is the worst case for defender-optics: it implies the audit trail exists when it doesn't. Cross-check: WIN-047/048/052/054 also point to Part 6.13 and have the same defect.

holes_found.description: 'Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax' (data/wins.json:WIN-055:detail_evidence, second-to-last sentence) is historically imprecise. Henrietta Leavitt's 1912 paper ('Periods of 25 Variable Stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud', Harvard College Observatory Circular 173) used Small Magellanic Cloud Cepheids, not LMC. Modern PL calibration uses LMC Cepheids (anchored via DEBs) AND MW Cepheids (anchored via Gaia parallaxes) as independent rungs, with the SMC playing a supporting role at lower metallicity. The compressed parenthetical conflates the original 1912 SMC discovery with the modern LMC calibration anchor. Not blocking — the substantive claim (Cepheid PL is calibrated via geometric distance anchors, not cosmological assumptions) is correct — but a careful defender could nitpick the historical detail.
holes_found.recommendation: Either drop the parenthetical entirely (it adds no argument-value) or rewrite as: 'The period-luminosity relation (Leavitt 1912, discovered in SMC; modern calibration anchored to the LMC distance from eclipsing binaries and Milky Way Cepheid parallaxes from Gaia)'. The shorter form is preferable. If hole #1 (Magellanic parallax) is fixed in the same edit pass, this can be combined into one citation-tightening rewrite.

stronger_arguments.description: TRGB independence as a SC amplifier. The detail_evidence mentions TRGB (Freedman 2019 CCHP) but doesn't fully exploit its rhetorical power. TRGB uses the helium-flash luminosity cutoff in old, low-metallicity halo stars — completely independent stellar physics from Cepheid radial pulsation or SBF surface-brightness statistics. The dome would need THREE independent invented mechanisms (one for pulsation, one for SBF magnitudes, one for the helium-flash luminosity cutoff) to explain why all three rungs agree. The SC argument becomes 'either the dome invents three independent mechanisms with no derivation, or the firmament-bounded geometry is wrong.' This is a stronger rhetorical close than the current text provides.
sources: Beaton et al. 2016 (Carnegie Hubble Program TRGB calibration)

stronger_arguments.description: Anand+Tully+Karachentsev 2021 (ApJ 932, 15) re-derived H0=71.5 from the same TRGB rungs with revised reddening corrections — a *second* TRGB-based H0 value that disagrees with Freedman's 69.8 in the third decimal but agrees on the megaparsec distance scale. Citing both reinforces the 'distance scale is robust even when H0 calibrators argue' framing, which preempts a 'TRGB is contested' counterargument.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model's underlying observation (that something correlates redshift with apparent distance) is not wrong — the Hubble-law correlation is real and measured. The dome's z=D/λ_A formula, when fit to the same data the standard ΛCDM Hubble-law uses, will produce numerically similar results because algebraically the formulas are isomorphic with λ_A playing the role of c/H0 (data/wins.json:WIN-055:sub_rationale; WIN-047 cross-reference). The dome's instinct that there should be a length scale (λ_A) in the redshift formula instead of a velocity is a defensible aesthetic preference and aligns with some redshift-as-property proposals in the alt-cosmology literature (tired-light, intrinsic redshift, etc.).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Adopting Hubble's Law with renamed constants is not 'predicting' the distance-redshift relation — it is restating it (data/wins.json:WIN-055:code_analysis.relabels_standard=true). And the Cepheid/SBF distance ladder is itself geometric (parallax + standard-candle physics), so claiming the dome 'predicts' Cepheid distances requires either (a) a mechanism for Cepheid pulsation periods under aetheric geometry (the dome has none — data/wins.json:WIN-055:code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false), or (b) accepting that galaxies actually exist at 10–100 Mpc (which falsifies the bounded firmament). The kernel of being right that 'something is going on with redshift' does not survive the choice between two bad options.

deeper_analysis: The verdict-change integration (Misleading → Self-Contradicted) is clean at the wins.json level — verdict, sub_rationale, and the EXP-295-rewritten tldr_verdict all landed via commit 5603fce1 on 2026-05-10. The substantive argument is unchanged from c3: the dome cites galactic-scale measurements as confirmation while its own bounded firmament denies the spatial framework. That argument holds. But two of the three c3 carry-over holes persist (sub_rationale rendering, Part 6.13 integration), and a fresh adversarial pass finds the 'Magellanic parallax' wording as a new moderate finding that the c3 reviewer didn't surface — likely because c3 was focused on the EXP-295 reclassification mechanics, not on a fresh attack on the detail_evidence prose itself. The current state is a partial integration: the verdict label says one thing, the on-page rationale says nothing, and the audit-trail section (Part 6.13) cited inside sub_rationale doesn't exist. A dome defender visiting the site today and reading WIN-055 sees 'Self-Contradicted' as a label and the same detail_evidence prose that earlier supported a 'Misleading' verdict, with no on-page bridge between them. That's the central optic problem.

citations_verified: doi.org/10.3847/2041-8213/ac5c5b → Riess et al. 2022 SH0ES (ApJL 934, L7) — valid DOI for the SH0ES H0 paper
doi.org/10.3847/1538-4357/abf81c → Blakeslee et al. 2021 SBF survey — valid DOI for the IR SBF Hubble constant paper
doi.org/10.3847/1538-4357/ab2f73 → Freedman et al. 2019 CCHP TRGB ApJ 882, 34 — valid DOI; H0=69.8 ± 0.8(stat) ± 1.7(sys), combined uncertainty ≈ 1.88 km/s/Mpc, our text's '± 1.9' rounds correctly.
citations_failed: 'Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax' — see hole #4. Leavitt's 1912 paper used SMC, not LMC. The 'LMC parallax' framing also misrepresents the LMC distance scale (DEBs, not parallax).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A sophisticated dome defender (one who has read our methodological principles and accepts internal-consistency-first) would argue: 'The dome model has never published an explicit statement that no spatial framework exists beyond the firmament. The 9,086 km firmament height is a measurement boundary for *known* dome geometry, not a claim that the universe ends there. The aether field that fills the cavity could plausibly extend outward with as-yet-unspecified topology; the dome is silent on extra-firmamental geometry, not denying it. Cepheid period-luminosity relations are measurements of pulsation periods and apparent magnitudes — they do not directly measure distance, they require a *theory* (the calibrated PL relation) to convert period+magnitude into distance. Under dome geometry, that theory needs re-derivation, and the resulting megaparsec values may not correspond to literal distances in dome coordinates. The Self-Contradicted verdict assumes the dome is committed to spherical-cosmology distance interpretations of Cepheid/SBF/TRGB data, but no such commitment is explicit in the dome literature. Self-Contradicted requires demonstrating a contradiction between two explicit dome commitments; here we have one explicit commitment (bounded firmament) and one interpretive *assumption* (that 10-100 Mpc distances are literal). Misleading was the correct verdict; the SC reclassification is premature.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence (or to the EXP-295 Part 6.13 subsection when integrated) explicitly addressing the 'dome is silent on extra-firmamental geometry' move: 'The dome cannot evade this contradiction by claiming silence on extra-firmamental geometry. WIN-055 itself adopts the standard-candle distance scale as confirmation of the dome's redshift formula — that adoption is the dome's explicit commitment to literal Mpc distances. A dome that endorsed Mpc-scale Cepheid distances at the wins.html level cannot then deny those distances at the geometry level without internal contradiction. The contradiction is between WIN-055 and the model's published firmament parameters, both of which the dome owns.' This closes the silence-defense by pointing to the WIN page itself as the load-bearing commitment.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: All five EXP-295 reclassified WINs use compatible Self-Contradicted logic (bounded firmament vs cited extragalactic distances). However, the on-page surfacing of the rationale is inconsistent — WIN-047 uses '30,000 km' in tldr_verdict where WIN-052/054/055 use '9,086 km' (carry-over from c3 hole #2; not re-attacked here because (i) c3 already flagged it and recommended it be a separate downstream tightening pass on WIN-047 specifically, and (ii) WIN-055's tldr_verdict is internally consistent with WIN-052/054). The sub_rationale-rendering and Part-6.13-integration defects apply uniformly across all five reclassified WINs, so the inconsistency is in the *infrastructure* not the *argument*. Decider's action on holes #2 and #3 here should be cross-applied to the other four.

claims_checked: Galaxies at 10–100 Mpc vs firmament at 9,086 km — orders-of-magnitude framing
30–300 million light-years parenthetical conversion of 10–100 Mpc
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) 10 Mpc = 10 × 3.0857e16 km = 3.0857e17 km. Ratio to firmament: 3.0857e17 / 9086 ≈ 3.4e13 → ~13.5 orders of magnitude. The phrase 'orders of magnitude beyond' (detail_evidence, tldr_verdict) is correct; specifically the gap is ~13 orders of magnitude in km units. (2) 1 pc = 3.262 light-years, so 10 Mpc = 32.62 Mly and 100 Mpc = 326 Mly. Our text's '30–300 million light-years' is the right order of magnitude and a defensible round. (3) Freedman 2019 reports H0 = 69.8 ± 0.8(stat) ± 1.7(sys). Combined in quadrature: sqrt(0.64 + 2.89) = sqrt(3.53) = 1.88 km/s/Mpc. Our text's '69.8 ± 1.9' is the combined-uncertainty form, rounds correctly. All three numerical claims verify.

summary_for_decider: WIN-055 c4 review: verdict change Misleading→Self-Contradicted holds under fresh attack (confidence 0.85). Three holes total; recommended_action='minor_edit'. (a) NEW moderate: 'Magellanic parallax' wording is technically wrong — LMC distance comes from detached eclipsing binaries (Pietrzynski 2013, 2019), not parallax. Specific replacement text in recommendation. Easy patch. (b) CARRY-OVER moderate from c3 hole #1: sub_rationale field is not rendered on the live site; reader sees 'Self-Contradicted' label with no surfaced rationale; ISS-1922 referenced in c3 turned out to be unrelated (about WIN-033). Carry-over hole detected but no matching open-issue found; decider should create one. Same defect on WIN-047/048/052/054 — recommend cross-applied fix. (c) CARRY-OVER moderate from c3 hole #0: Part 6.13 (EXP-295 cross-cutting subsection) still not integrated; the 'See Part 6.13' cross-reference inside the sub_rationales (also same on WIN-047/048/052/054) points at a non-existent section. The 8,619 km wording-fix HNOTE WAS applied to the EXP file on 2026-05-10 (resolves the c3 numerical-error finding inside EXP-295), but integration itself never happened. EXP-295.status=null, EXP-295.integrated_at=null. Carry-over hole detected but no matching open-issue found; decider should create one. (d) Minor: 'Leavitt 1912 LMC' historical conflation (her 1912 paper used SMC). Lens A (Advocate): defense_survives=3 (the 'dome is silent on extra-firmamental geometry' defense has rhetorical traction; preemption text supplied). Lens B (cross-WIN): compatible with WIN-047/048/052/054; rendering-infrastructure defects propagate across all five. Lens C (quant): all numerical claims verified. Citations: 3/4 valid DOIs; the 'Leavitt 1912, LMC parallax' parenthetical is the only failure.
